findings.title: Regex patterns are more restrictive than original find() — behavior difference is undocumented but benign
findings.description: The old code used `lower.find('<head')` which would match any HTML-like string starting with '<head', including '<headings>', '<header>', etc. The new patterns `r'<head[\s>]'`, `r'<html[\s>]'`, `r'<!doctype[\s>]'` require either whitespace or '>' immediately after the tag name. This is semantically more correct for HTML tag matching and eliminates false positives on non-standard tag names. In practice this is an improvement, but the behavioral change is not mentioned in the task spec and there are no regression tests covering inputs like '<headings>' or '<header>'.
findings.impact: Negligible. In valid HTML the only tag that would start with '<head' is '<head>' or '<head '. No real-world HTML page uses '<headings>' as a root element. The change is safer than the original.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Optionally add a comment noting that the regex is intentionally more precise than a bare `find('<head')` to prevent false matches on tags like '<header>'. No test gap needs filling given the hot-path is well-covered by 237 passing tests.

hardcoded_secrets: None found. No API keys, passwords, tokens, or encoded credentials in diff.

owasp_coverage.A01_broken_access_control: PASS — is_target_domain() logic is semantically equivalent to previous implementation. All TARGET_DOMAINS start with '.', ensuring endswith() cannot be tricked into matching attacker-controlled superdomain (e.g., 'fakegoogle.com'.endswith('.google.com') = False).
owasp_coverage.A02_cryptographic_failures: PASS — No cryptographic changes. secrets.token_urlsafe(16) nonce generation unchanged.
owasp_coverage.A03_injection: PASS — All three regex patterns are static string literals, not derived from user input. No injection risk.
owasp_coverage.A04_insecure_design: PASS — No design changes. Pure internal refactor.
owasp_coverage.A05_security_misconfiguration: PASS — No configuration, headers, or CORS changes.
owasp_coverage.A06_vulnerable_components: PASS — Only new dependency is stdlib `re` module.
owasp_coverage.A07_auth_failures: PASS — No authentication or session changes.
owasp_coverage.A08_software_integrity: PASS — ReDoS analysis: patterns `<head[\s>]`, `<html[\s>]`, `<!doctype[\s>]` are fixed-width with character-class quantifiers only. No nested quantifiers, no catastrophic backtracking possible.
owasp_coverage.A10_ssrf: PASS — No URL handling or HTTP client calls introduced.
